prompt_text: Give a weekly diet and workout routine.
response_text:
Here's a weekly diet and workout routine that you can follow. Please note that this is a general plan and may need to be adjusted based on your individual needs, fitness goals, and health requirements.

**Diet Plan:**

This diet plan is focused on providing a balanced mix of nutrients, with an emphasis on whole foods, fruits, vegetables, lean proteins, and healthy fats.

**Monday:**

* Breakfast: Oatmeal with banana, almond milk, and walnuts (400 calories)
* Snack: Apple slices with peanut butter (150 calories)
* Lunch: Grilled chicken breast with quinoa, broccoli, and avocado (500 calories)
* Snack: Greek yogurt with berries (150 calories)
* Dinner: Baked salmon with sweet potato, green beans, and brown rice (500 calories)

**Tuesday:**

* Breakfast: Scrambled eggs with whole wheat toast, spinach, and cherry tomatoes (300 calories)
* Snack: Carrot sticks with hummus (100 calories)
* Lunch: Turkey and avocado wrap with mixed greens (500 calories)
* Snack: Cottage cheese with cucumber slices (150 calories)
* Dinner: Grilled chicken breast with roasted vegetables and quinoa (500 calories)

**Wednesday:**

* Breakfast: Smoothie bowl with banana, spinach, almond milk, and almond butter (400 calories)
* Snack: Hard-boiled egg and cherry tomatoes (100 calories)
* Lunch: Grilled chicken breast with mixed greens and whole wheat pita (500 calories)
* Snack: Rice cakes with almond butter and banana slices (150 calories)
* Dinner: Baked chicken thighs with roasted Brussels sprouts and brown rice (500 calories)

**Thursday:**

* Breakfast: Avocado toast with scrambled eggs and whole wheat bread (350 calories)
* Snack: Protein bar (120 calories)
* Lunch: Grilled chicken Caesar salad (500 calories)
* Snack: Cottage cheese with fruit (150 calories)
* Dinner: Grilled shrimp with quinoa, asparagus, and brown rice (500 calories)

**Friday:**

* Breakfast: Overnight oats with almond milk, chia seeds, and berries (400 calories)
* Snack: Apple slices with almond butter (150 calories)
* Lunch: Turkey and cheese sandwich on whole wheat bread with carrot sticks (500 calories)
* Snack: Greek yogurt with honey (150 calories)
* Dinner: Baked chicken breast with roasted vegetables and quinoa (500 calories)

**Saturday:**

* Breakfast: Breakfast burrito with scrambled eggs, black beans, and avocado (400 calories)
* Snack: Protein smoothie with banana, spinach, and almond milk (200 calories)
* Lunch: Grilled chicken breast with mixed greens and whole wheat wrap (500 calories)
* Snack: Carrot sticks with hummus (100 calories)
* Dinner: Grilled salmon with roasted asparagus and quinoa (500 calories)

**Sunday:**

* Breakfast: Breakfast tacos with scrambled eggs, black beans, and avocado (400 calories)
* Snack: Rice cakes with almond butter and banana slices (150 calories)
* Lunch: Grilled chicken breast with mixed greens and whole wheat pita (500 calories)
* Snack: Greek yogurt with berries (150 calories)
* Dinner: Baked chicken thighs with roasted Brussels sprouts and brown rice (500 calories)

**Workout Routine:**

This workout routine is designed to provide a balanced mix of cardio, strength training, and flexibility exercises.

**Monday (Chest and Triceps):**

* Warm-up: 5-10 minutes of cardio (jogging, jumping jacks, etc.)
* Barbell bench press (3 sets of 8-12 reps)
* Incline dumbbell press (3 sets of 10-15 reps)
* Tricep pushdown (3 sets of 12-15 reps)
* Tricep dips (3 sets of 12-15 reps)
* Cool-down: 5-10 minutes of stretching

**Tuesday (Back and Biceps):**

* Warm-up: 5-10 minutes of cardio
* Pull-ups (3 sets of 8-12 reps)
* Barbell rows (3 sets of 8-12 reps)
* Dumbbell bicep curls (3 sets of 10-15 reps)
* Hammer curls (3 sets of 10-15 reps)
* Cool-down: 5-10 minutes of stretching

**Wednesday (Rest Day)**

**Thursday (Legs):**

* Warm-up: 5-10 minutes of cardio
* Squats (3 sets of 8-12 reps)
* Leg press (3 sets of 10-15 reps)
* Lunges (3 sets of 10-15 reps)
* Leg extensions (3 sets of 12-15 reps)
* Cool-down: 5-10 minutes of stretching

**Friday (Shoulders and Abs):**

* Warm-up: 5-10 minutes of cardio
* Dumbbell shoulder press (3 sets of 8-12 reps)
* Lateral raises (3 sets of 10-15 reps)
* Rear delt fly (3 sets of 12-15 reps)
* Plank (3 sets of 30-60 seconds)
* Russian twists (3 sets of 10-15 reps)
* Cool-down: 5-10 minutes of stretching

**Saturday (Cardio):**

* Warm-up: 5-10 minutes of cardio
* High-intensity interval training (HIIT) (30-45 minutes)
* Cool-down: 5-10 minutes of stretching

**Sunday (Rest Day)**

Remember to stay hydrated by drinking plenty of water throughout the day, and to listen to your body and adjust the diet and workout routine as needed. It's also important to consult with a healthcare professional or registered dietitian before making any significant changes to your diet or exercise routine.
